text.title: Direnv + Mise for Per-Project Environments Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Direnv + Mise for Per-Project Environments` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every repo MUST commit `.mise.toml` pinning language + version (e.g. `python = "3.12"`). Uncommitted version = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without a committed manifest, every collaborator (incl. CI) installs a different version; reproducibility dies.

rule.statement: Every Python repo MUST commit `.envrc` that runs `use mise` and exports the virtualenv path. Missing .envrc = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without auto-load, `cd repo && python` may run the wrong interpreter; manual `mise use` is forgotten.

rule.statement: Tools installed via mise MUST live under `~/.local/share/mise/installs/`, not /usr/local. Global installs = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Global installs in /usr/local override every repo's pinned version; mise is opt-in per shim.

rule.statement: `mise trust` MUST be called per-repo to allow .mise.toml. Auto-trust globally = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Auto-trusting any .mise.toml means a malicious clone executes arbitrary pre-script. Per-repo trust is the security boundary.

rule.statement: CI MUST install via the same `.mise.toml` (e.g. via mise GitHub Action) so dev + CI run identical versions. Hand-coded CI version = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Hand-coded CI matrix drifts from local .mise.toml; 'works on my machine' bugs follow.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
